item.title: OSM for sustainable transport planning: getting started
item.description:
Abstract

This workshop is aimed at everyone interested in using OpenStreetMap (OSM) to support sustainable transport planning, in professional or advocacy contexts. The focus will be on getting started, identifying, visualizing, and analyzing key tags and identifying gaps in walking, cycling, and wheeling networks. Participants will place OSM data in the context of other data sources to identify its unique advantages. The workshop will be practical and get users started with key ‘tools of the trade’ in the areas: R (used for live demo), Python, A/B Street, and osm2streets. By the end of the workshop, you will know how to add value to OSM data to support sustainable transport planning.

item.title: The potential of OpenStreetMap for (accessible) active travel planning
Propensity to Cycle Tool Advanced Training Workshop
item.description:
Abstract

The focus here is on analysing cycling potential in the open source statistical programming language R. We use R because the PCT was developed in, and can be extended with, R code. Using open source software with a command-line interface reduces barriers to entry, enabling the development of open access transport models for more citizen-led and participatory transport planning, such as integration with the A/B Street city simulation and editing software (Lovelace 2021). To view a video of our previous advanced training workshop at the Cycle Active City 2021 Conference, see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OiLzjrBMQmU.

item.title: New data products for local authorities
item.description:
Abstract

A new project led by the University of Leeds’ called openinfra is aiming to transform the evidence-base about infrastructure provision as it relates to walking and cycling. With input from the Department for Transport, Ordnance Survey and West Yorkshire Combined Authority, openinfra will explore the potential for open datasets, assess their strengths and weaknesses and teach best practices in using them. https://github.com/udsleeds/openinfra
